cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo These tests exercise the full HTTP data-plane router through three lenses:\\n+//!\\n+//! 1. **Optional JWT layer (ADR-018 cutover)**: requests without an\\n+//!    `Authorization` header fall through to the legacy no-auth path; requests\\n+//!    WITH a valid JWT are fully verified by `jwt_verify_layer`.\\n+//!\\n+//! 2. **Audit attribution**: after a JWT-authenticated write, the resulting\\n+//!    audit entry has its `attribution` field populated.\\n+//!\\n+//! 3. **gRPC composite header**: the renamed `x-axon-tenant-database` header\\n+//!    carries `{tenant}:{database}` and the gRPC helper extracts only the\\n+//!    database component.\\n+\\n+#![allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]\\n+\\n+use std::path::PathBuf;\\n+use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\n+use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n+\\n+use serde_json::{json, Value};\\n+use mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n+    storage.insert_user(User {\\n+        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n+        display_name: \\\"Cutover User\\\".to_string(),\\n+        email: None,\\n+        created_at_ms: 0,\\n+        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n+    });\\n+    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n+        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n+        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n+        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n+    });\\n+    storage\\n+}\\n+\\n+/// Build a `ControlPlaneState` with jwt_issuer + auth storage so the optional\\n+/// JWT verification layer is enabled in the HTTP router.\\n+fn make_control_plane(issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e, auth_storage: MemoryStorageAdapter) -\u003e ControlPlaneState {\\n+    let db = ControlPlaneDb::open_in_memory().expect(\\\"in-memory control-plane db should open\\\");\\n+    let db = Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(db));\\n+    let boxed: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e = Box::new(auth_storage);\\n+    let storage_arc: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e =\\n+        Arc::new(Mutex::new(boxed));\\n+    ControlPlaneState::new(db, PathBuf::from(\\\".\\\"), UserRoleStore::default(), CorsStore::default())\\n+        .with_jwt_issuer(issuer)\\n+        .with_storage(storage_arc)\\n+}\\n+\\n+/// Build an make_server(\\n+    issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n+) -\u003e (axum_test::TestServer, TenantHandler) {\\n+    let auth_storage = make_auth_storage();\\n+    let control_plane = make_control_plane(issuer, auth_storage);\\n+\\n+    let data_storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e =\\n+        Box::new(MemoryStorageAdapter::default());\\n+    let handler: TenantHandler =\\n+        Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(AxonHandler::new(data_storage)));\\n+    let tenant_router = Arc::new(TenantRouter::single(handler.clone()));\\n+\\n+    let app = build_router_with_auth(\\n+        tenant_router,\\n+        \\\"memory\\\",\\n+        None,\\n+        AuthContext::no_auth(),\\n+        RateLimitConfig::default(),\\n+ vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n+            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n+            ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\\n+        }],\\n+    }\\n+}\\n+\\n+fn read_only_grants() -\u003e Grants {\\n+    Grants {\\n+        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n+            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n+            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n+        }],\\n+    }\\n+}\\n+\\n+fn make_token(issuer: TENANT.to_string(),\\n+        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n+        iat: now,\\n+        nbf: now,\\n+        exp: now + 3600,\\n+        grants,\\n+    };\\n+    issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"token issuance should succeed\\\")\\n+}\\n+\\n+fn entity_path(id: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n+    format!(\\\"/tenants/{TENANT}/databases/{DATABASE}/entities/{COLLECTION}/{id}\\\")\\n+}\\n+\\n+fn entity_body(value: i32) -\u003e Value {\\n+    json!({ \\\"data\\\": { \\\"value\\\": value } = issuer();\\n+    let (server, _handler) = make_server(jw);\\n+\\n+    // No Authorization header → goes through no-auth path → 201\\n+    let resp = server\\n+        .post(\u0026entity_path(\\\"e-noauth-01\\\"))\\n+        .json(\u0026entity_body(1))\\n+        .await;\\n+    resp.assert_status(axum::http::StatusCode::CREATED);\\n+}\\n+\\n+// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with write grant → 201 ─────────────────────────────────\\n+\\n+#[tokio::test]\\n+async fn cutover_valid_jwt_write_grant_creates_entity_201() {\\n+    let jw = issuer();\\n+    let token = make_token(\u0026jw, write_grants());\\n+    let (server, _handler) = make_server(jw);\\n+\\n+    let resp = server\\n+        .post(\u0026entity_path(\\\"e-jwt-write-01\\\"))\\n+ assert_eq!(body[\\\"entity\\\"][\\\"id\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"), \\\"e-jwt-write-01\\\");\\n+}\\n+\\n+// ── Test 3: JWT with read-only grant blocks POST → 403 ───────────────────────\\n+\\n+#[tokio::test]\\n+async fn cutover_jwt_read_only_grant_blocks_post_403() {\\n+    let jw = issuer();\\n+    let token = make_token(\u0026jw, read_only_grants());\\n+    let (server, _handler) = make_server(jw);\\n+\\n+    let resp = server\\n+        .post(\u0026entity_path(\\\"e-jwt-read-01\\\"))\\n+        .add_header(\\n+            axum::http::header::AUTHORIZATION,\\n+            axum::http::HeaderValue::from_str(\u0026format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\")).unwrap(),\\n+        )\\n+        .json(\u0026entity_body(1))\\n+ .await;\\n+    resp.assert_status(axum::http::StatusCode::CREATED);\\n+\\n+    // Query the audit log directly from the data-plane handler.\\n+    let audit_resp = handler\\n+        .lock()\\n+ cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! # Verification order\\n//!\\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\\n\\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::extract::State;\\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\\nuse serde_json::json;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\\npub struct JwtIssuer {\\n    secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e,\\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\\n    pub issuer_id: String,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JwtIssuer {\\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n    pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n        encode(\\n            \u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\n            claims,\\n            \u0026EncodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret),\\n        )\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n    ///\\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n    pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\\n\\n        decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature =\u003e AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n            })\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\\n///\\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    inner: RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    /// Create an empty cache.\\n    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n        Self::default()\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\\n    pub fn contains(\u0026self, jti: \u0026Uuid) -\u003e bool {\\n        self.inner\\n            .read()\\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\\n            .unwrap_or(false)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\\n    pub fn insert(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) {\\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\\n            g.insert(jti);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\\n///\\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\\n#[derive(Clone)]\\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\\n    pub issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc\u003cInMemoryRevocationCache\u003e,\\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\\n    pub storage: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Convenience constructor.\\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self {\\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\\n///\\n/// Install with:\\n/// ```ignore\\n/// Router::new()\\n///     .route(\\\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\\\", …)\\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n///         Arc::new(state),\\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\\n///     ))\\n/// ```\\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\\n    State(state): State\u003cArc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e\u003e,\\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\\n    next: Next,\\n) -\u003e Response {\\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\\n\\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(\u0026path) else {\\n        return next.run(request).await;\\n    };\\n\\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\\n    let database = database.to_string();\\n    let method = request.method().clone();\\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\\n\\n    match verify_request(\u0026state, \u0026headers, \u0026method, \u0026tenant, \u0026database) {\\n        Ok(identity) =\u003e {\\n            let mut request = request;\\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\\n            next.run(request).await\\n        }\\n        Err(err) =\u003e AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfn verify_request(\\n    state: \u0026AuthPipelineState,\\n    headers: \u0026HeaderMap,\\n    method: \u0026Method,\\n    tenant: \u0026str,\\n    database: \u0026str,\\n) -\u003e Result\u003cResolvedIdentity, AuthError\u003e {\\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\\n\\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\\n\\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs();\\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\\n    // Expired: exp + skew \u003c now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) \u003c now {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\\n    }\\n    // Not yet valid: nbf \u003e now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\\n    if claims.nbf \u003e now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\\n        .jti\\n        .parse()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(\u0026jti_uuid) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n    }\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        if storage\\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\\n        {\\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\\n            drop(storage);\\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\\n    let user_id = UserId::new(\u0026claims.sub);\\n    let user = {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n    };\\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\\n    let granted_db = claims\\n        .grants\\n        .find_database(database)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\\n\\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\\n        Op::Read\\n    } else {\\n        Op::Write\\n    };\\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\\n    }\\n\\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\\n        user_id,\\n        tenant_id,\\n        grants: claims.grants,\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Extract the `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` value from the `Authorization` header.\\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: \u0026HeaderMap) -\u003e Result\u003c\u0026str, AuthError\u003e {\\n    let auth_header = headers\\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n\\n    let auth_str = auth_header\\n        .to_str()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n\\n    auth_str\\n        .strip_prefix(\\\"Bearer \\\")\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n}\\n\\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\\n///\\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e {\\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\\n        (Some(\\\"tenants\\\"), Some(tenant), Some(\\\"databases\\\"), Some(rest))\\n            if !tenant.is_empty() \u0026\u0026 !rest.is_empty() =\u003e\\n        {\\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\\n            if database.is_empty() {\\n                None\\n            } else {\\n                Some((tenant, database))\\n            }\\n        }\\n        _ =\u003e None,\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\\n\\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\\n    fn into_response(self) -\u003e Response {\\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\\n        let body = json!({\\n            \\\"error\\\": {\\n                \\\"code\\\": self.0.error_code(),\\n                \\\"message\\\": self.0.to_string()\\n            }\\n        });\\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\\n \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"u-integration-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"integration-test-secret\\\";\\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Integration User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\\n            Box::new(storage) as Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\\n        )),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service should not fail\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .unwrap_or_default();\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer(token: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_token(issuer: \u0026JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -\u003e String {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants,\\n    };\\n    issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn orders_read_grants() -\u003e Grants {\\n    Grants {\\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n        }],\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"unauthenticated\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test token = valid_token(\u0026state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions.     15	func TestVersion_StaleWarning_PrintsToStderr(t *testing.T) {
    16		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
    17	
    18		repoDir := initVersionTestRepo(t, "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.git")
    19		initialSHA := gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "README.md", "# initial\n", "initial")
    20		_ = gitCommitFile(t, repoDir, "notes.txt", "stale\n", "stale")
    21	
    22		stdout, stderr, err := runVersionCommand(t, repoDir, "v1.2.3", initialSHA)
    23		require.NoError(t, err)
    24	
    25		assert.Contains(t, stdout, "DDx v1.2.3")
    26		assert.Contains(t, stderr, "WARNING: installed ddx is built from")
    27		assert.Contains(t, stderr, "commits ahead")
    28		assert.Contains(t, stderr, "Run \"make install\" to refresh.")
    29	}

    31	func TestVersion_NoWarningWhenInSync(t *testing.T) {

    33

    40		assert.Empty(t, stderr)
    41	}

    55	
    56	func TestVersion_HandlesDetachedHead(t *testing.T) {

    76	func initVersionTestRepo(t *testing.T, originURL string) string {

    87	func gitCommitFile(t *testing.T, repoDir, relPath, content, commitMsg string) string {
    88		t.Helper()
    89	
    90		fullPath := filepath.Join(repoDir, relPath)
    91		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(fullPath), 0o755))
    92		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(fullPath, []byte(content), 0o644))
    93		runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "add", relPath)
    94		runVersionGit(t, repoDir, "commit", "-m", commitMsg)
    95		return runVersionGitOutput(t, repoDir, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
    96	}

     9	)
    10	
    11	var version = "dev"
    12	var commit = "unknown"
    13	var date = "unknown"
    14	
    15	func main() {
    16		// Set version info for cobra
    17		cmd.Version = version
    18		cmd.Commit = commit
    19		cmd.Date = date
    20	
    21		// Get working directory once at startup, then resolve to the git
    22		// repository root so that ddx always operates from the project root
    23		// regardless of which subdirectory the user invokes it from.
    24		cwd, err := os.Getwd()
    25		if err != nil {
    26			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error getting working directory: %v\n", err)
    27			os.Exit(1)
    28		}
    29		workingDir := git.FindProjectRoot(cwd)
    30	
    31		if err := cmd.Execute(workingDir); err != nil {
    32			// Check if it's an ExitError with a specific exit code
    33			if exitErr, ok := err.(*cmd.ExitError); ok {
    34				// Print error message only if it's not empty
    35				if exitErr.Message != "" {
    36					fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: %v\n", exitErr.Message)
    37				}
    38				os.Exit(exitErr.Code)
    39			}
    40			// For other errors, print them
    41			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: %v\n", err)
    42			os.Exit(1)
    43		}
    44	}

   134		}
   135		rootCmd.SetVersionTemplate(versionTemplate)

   139		rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVarP(&verbose, "verbose", "v", false, "verbose output")

data.text: The root command already has version templating and a `--no-check` escape hatch, which makes this likely a small patch in `command_factory.go` plus focused tests. I’m reading the version command registration and the repo/git helpers before editing.

data.output:
1	package git
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"context"
     5		"os"
     6		"path/filepath"
     7		"strings"
     8		"time"
     9	)
    10	
    11	// FindProjectRoot walks up from startDir to find the git repository root.
    12	// It returns the top-level directory of the git working tree. If startDir
    13	// is not inside a git repository, it returns startDir unchanged.
    14	//
    15	// This is analogous to `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` and ensures that
    16	// ddx always operates from the repository root regardless of the caller's
    17	// working directory. Without this, running `ddx` from a subdirectory that
    18	// contains its own `.ddx/` folder would silently use the wrong workspace.
    19	func FindProjectRoot(startDir string) string {
    20		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
    21		defer cancel()
    22	
    23		cmd := Command(ctx, startDir, "rev-parse", "--show-toplevel")
    24		out, err := cmd.Output()
    25		if err != nil {
    26			// Not in a git repo — fall back to the original directory.
    27			return startDir
    28		}
    29		root := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
    30		if root == "" {
    31			return startDir
    32		}
    33		return root
    34	}
    35	
    36	// FindNearestDDxWorkspace walks up from startDir to find the nearest ancestor
    37	// inside the current git repository that contains a .ddx workspace.
    38	//
    39	// When startDir is inside a linked git worktree (e.g. an execute-bead
    40	// isolated worktree under .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* or a worktrunk sibling
    41	// like repo.feature-branch/), it resolves to the PRIMARY worktree's .ddx/
    42	// first, not the linked worktree's own .ddx/. This is critical because:
    43	//
    44	//   - bead store mutations must land in the canonical project bead queue,
    45	//     not in an isolated worktree's private snapshot that will be discarded
    46	//   - the primary worktree is the operator's source of truth; linked
    47	//     worktrees are ephemeral execution contexts
    48	//
    49	// If the primary worktree has no .ddx/ (or we aren't in a linked worktree),
    50	// it falls back to walking up from startDir within the current git
    51	// repository.
    52	//
    53	// Returns an empty string if no .ddx/ is found.
    54	func FindNearestDDxWorkspace(startDir string) string {
    55		abs, err := filepath.Abs(startDir)
    56		if err != nil {
    57			return ""
    58		}
    59	
    60		// If we're inside a linked worktree, prefer the primary worktree's .ddx/.
    61		if primary := primaryWorktreeRoot(abs); primary != "" && primary != FindProjectRoot(abs) {
    62			candidate := filepath.Join(primary, ".ddx")
    63			if info, statErr := os.Stat(candidate); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() {
    64				return primary
    65			}
    66		}
    67	
    68		gitRoot := FindProjectRoot(abs)
    69		current := abs
    70		for {
    71			candidate := filepath.Join(current, ".ddx")
    72			if info, statErr := os.Stat(candidate); statErr == nil && info.IsDir() {
    73				return current
    74			}
    75			if current == gitRoot {
    76				break
    77			}
    78			parent := filepath.Dir(current)
    79			if parent == current {
    80				break
    81			}
    82			current = parent
    83		}
    84		return ""
    85	}
    86	
    87	// IsRepository checks if the current directory is a git repository.
    88	func IsRepository(path string) bool {
    89		if path == "" {
    90			path = "."
    91		}
    92	
    93		cleanPath := filepath.Clean(path)
    94		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
    95		defer cancel()
    96	
    97		cmd := Command(ctx, cleanPath, "rev-parse", "--git-dir")
    98		return cmd.Run() == nil
    99	}
   100	
   101	// primaryWorktreeRoot returns the primary (non-linked) worktree directory
   102	// for a given path inside a git repository, or "" if the path is not inside
   103	// a linked worktree (or if resolution fails).
   104	//
   105	// Detection: `git rev-parse --git-common-dir` returns the shared .git
   106	// directory. If that differs from `git rev-parse --git-dir`, we're in a
   107	// linked worktree. The primary worktree is the parent directory of the
   108	// common .git dir (unless the shared dir is a bare repo).
   109	func primaryWorktreeRoot(startDir string) string {
   110		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
   111		defer cancel()
   112	
   113		gitDirCmd := Command(ctx, startDir, "rev-parse", "--path-format=absolute", "--git-dir")
   114		gitDirOut, err := gitDirCmd.Output()
   115		if err != nil {
   116			return ""
   117		}
   118		gitDir := strings.TrimSpace(string(gitDirOut))
   119	
   120		commonDirCmd := Command(ctx, startDir, "rev-parse", "--path-format=absolute", "--git-common-dir")
   121		commonDirOut, err := commonDirCmd.Output()
   122		if err != nil {
   123			return ""
   124		}
   125		commonDir := strings.TrimSpace(string(commonDirOut))
   126	
   127		if gitDir == commonDir {
   128			// Not a linked worktree.
   129			return ""
   130		}
   131	
   132		// Common dir is either a bare repo or the primary worktree's .git dir.
   133		// If it's a .git directory, the primary worktree is its parent.
   134		if filepath.Base(commonDir) == ".git" {
   135			return filepath.Dir(commonDir)
   136		}
   137		// Bare repo: no primary worktree. Caller falls back to walk-up.
   138		return ""
   139	}

   476	// registerSubcommands adds all subcommands to the root command
   477	func (f *CommandFactory) registerSubcommands(rootCmd *cobra.Command) {
   478		// Version command
   479		versionCmd := &cobra.Command{
   480			Use:   "version",
   481			Short: "Show version information",
   482			Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
   483				_, _ = fmt.Fprint(cmd.OutOrStdout(), f.versionOutput())
   484				f.warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd)
   485	
   486				// Check for --no-check flag
   487				noCheck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-check")
   488				_ = noCheck // TODO: Implement update checking when this flag is used
   489				// For now, we don't check for updates
   490			},
   491		}
   492		versionCmd.Flags().Bool("no-check", false, "Skip checking for updates")
   493		rootCmd.AddCommand(versionCmd)
   494	
   495		// Completion command
   496		completionCmd := &cobra.Command{
   497			Use:   "completion [bash|zsh|fish|powershell]",
   498			Short: "Generate completion script",
   499			Long: `To configure your shell to load completions:
   500	
   501	Bash:
   502	  echo 'source <(ddx completion bash)' >> ~/.bashrc
   503	
   504	Zsh:
   505	  echo 'source <(ddx completion zsh)' >> ~/.zshrc
   506	
   507	Fish:
   508	  ddx completion fish | source
   509	
   510	PowerShell:
   511	  ddx completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression
   512	`,
   513			DisableFlagsInUseLine: true,
   514			ValidArgs:             []string{"bash", "zsh", "fish", "powershell"},
   515			Args:                  cobra.MatchAll(cobra.ExactArgs(1), cobra.OnlyValidArgs),
   516			Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
   517				switch args[0] {
   518				case "bash":
   519					_ = rootCmd.GenBashCompletion(os.Stdout)
   520				case "zsh":
   521					_ = rootCmd.GenZshCompletion(os.Stdout)
   522				case "fish":
   523					_ = rootCmd.GenFishCompletion(os.Stdout, true)
   524				case "powershell":
   525					_ = rootCmd.GenPowerShellCompletionWithDesc(os.Stdout)
   526				}
   527			},
   528		}
   529		rootCmd.AddCommand(completionCmd)
   530	
   531		// Register all other commands
   532		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInitCommand())
   533		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newListCommand())
   534		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newDoctorCommand())
   535		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newUpdateCommand())
   536		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newUpgradeCommand())
   537		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newConfigCommand())
   538		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newPersonaCommand())
   539		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newStatusCommand())
   540		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newLogCommand())
   541		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newBeadCommand())
   542		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newArtifactCommand())
   543		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newExecCommand())
   544		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newMetricCommand())
   545		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newMetricsCommand())
   546		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCommand())
   547		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newAcCommand())
   548		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newDocCommand())
   549		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newCheckpointCommand())
   550		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newServerCommand())
   551		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newSkillsCommand())
   552		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInstallCommand())
   553		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInstalledCommand())
   554		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newUninstallCommand())
   555		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newSearchCommand())
   556		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newOutdatedCommand())
   557		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newVerifyCommand())
   558		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newJqCommand())
   559		rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkCommand())

   563		// Add prompts command group
   564		promptsCmd := &cobra.Command{
   565			Use:     "prompts",
   566			Short:   "Manage AI prompts",
   567			Aliases: []string{"prompt"},
   568		}
   569		promptsCmd.AddCommand(f.newPromptsListCommand())

   574	func (f *CommandFactory) warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd *cobra.Command) {
   575		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
   576			return
   577		}
   578		if f.Commit == "" || f.Commit == "unknown" {
   579			return
   580		}
   581	
   582		repoRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
   583		if repoRoot == "" || !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
   584			return
   585		}
   586	
   587		originURL, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "remote", "get-url", "origin")
   588		if err != nil || !isDDXOriginURL(originURL) {
   589			return
   590		}
   591	
   592		buildSHA, err := gitResolveCommit(repoRoot, f.Commit)
   593		if err != nil {
   594			return
   595		}
   596		headSHA, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
   597		if err != nil {
   598			return
   599		}
   600	
   601		if err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), repoRoot, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", buildSHA, headSHA).Run(); err != nil {
   602			return
   603		}
   604	
   605		aheadCount, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-list", "--count", buildSHA+".."+headSHA)
   606		if err != nil || aheadCount == "0" {
   607			return
   608		}
   609	
   610		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(),
   611			"WARNING: installed ddx is built from %s; source tree HEAD is %s (%s commits ahead). Run \"make install\" to refresh.\n",
   612			buildSHA, headSHA, aheadCount)
   613	}
   614	
   615	func gitCommandOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
   616		out, err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).Output()
   617		if err != nil {
   618			return "", err
   619		}
   620		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil

     2	
     3	import (
     4		"os"
     5		"os/exec"
     6		"path/filepath"
     7		"strings"
     8		"testing"
     9	
    10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    12	)
    13	
    14	// TestMain scrubs GIT_* environment variables for the whole test process.
    15	// When the test suite is invoked from inside a lefthook pre-commit hook,
    16	// lefthook sets GIT_DIR, GIT_WORK_TREE, GIT_INDEX_FILE, GIT_AUTHOR_*,
    17	// GIT_COMMITTER_*, etc. to paths inside the *parent* repository. Any git
    18	// subprocess these tests spawn — whether via raw exec.Command or via the
    19	// production code under test — would otherwise inherit those variables
    20	// and mutate the parent repo's config (e.g. leaking a stray
    21	// `worktree = /tmp/TestXxx/001` line into the shared .git/config), which
    22	// then corrupts every subsequent git operation in the parent repo.
    23	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
    24		for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
    25			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
    26				if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
    27					_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
    28				}
    29			}
    30		}
    31		os.Exit(m.Run())
    32	}
    33	
    34	// scrubbedGitEnv returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
    35	// removed. When tests run inside a lefthook pre-commit hook, lefthook sets
    36	// GIT_DIR, GIT_WORK_TREE, GIT_INDEX_FILE, GIT_AUTHOR_*, GIT_COMMITTER_*, etc.
    37	// to the parent repo's paths. A child `git init` in a temp dir would inherit
    38	// those, making the child write to the PARENT repo's config (leaking a stray
    39	// `worktree = /tmp/TestXxx/001` line into the shared .git/config) and
    40	// corrupting the parent. Always use this helper for test-local git
    41	// subprocesses to keep them isolated.
    42	func scrubbedGitEnv() []string {
    43		parent := os.Environ()
    44		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
    45		for _, kv := range parent {
    46			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
    47				continue
    48			}
    49			env = append(env, kv)
    50		}
    51		return env
    52	}
    53	
    54	// runGitInDir runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env. Fails the
    55	// test if the command returns a non-zero exit status.
    56	func runGitInDir(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) {
    57		t.Helper()
    58		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
    59		cmd.Dir = dir
    60		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnv()
    61		if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
    62			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
    63		}
    64	}
    65	
    66	// Helper function to create a test git repository
    67	func setupTestGitRepo(t *testing.T) string {
    68		tempDir := t.TempDir()
    69	
    70		runGitInDir(t, tempDir, "init")
    71		runGitInDir(t, tempDir, "config", "user.email", "test@example.com")
    72		runGitInDir(t, tempDir, "config", "user.name", "Test User")
    73	
    74		// Create initial commit
    75		testFile := filepath.Join(tempDir, "README.md")
    76		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(testFile, []byte("# Test Repo"), 0644))
    77	
    78		runGitInDir(t, tempDir, "add", ".")
    79		runGitInDir(t, tempDir, "commit", "-m", "Initial commit")
    80	
    81		return tempDir
    82	}

    84	// TestIsRepository tests checking if a directory is a git repository
    85	func TestIsRepository_Basic(t *testing.T) {
    86		tests := []struct {
    87			name     string
    88			setup    func() string
    89			expected bool
    90		}{
    91			{
    92				name: "valid git repository",
    93				setup: func() string {
    94					return setupTestGitRepo(t)
    95				},
    96				expected: true,
    97			},
    98			{
    99				name: "non-git directory",
   100				setup: func() string {
   101					return t.TempDir()
   102				},
   103				expected: false,
   104			},
   105			{
   106				name: "non-existent directory",
   107				setup: func() string {
   108					return filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "nonexistent")
   109				},
   110				expected: false,
   111			},
   112		}
   113	
   114		for _, tt := range tests {
   115			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   116				path := tt.setup()
   117				result := IsRepository(path)
   118				assert.Equal(t, tt.expected, result)
   119			})
   120		}
   121	}

   123	// TestFindProjectRoot tests git root resolution
   124	func TestFindProjectRoot(t *testing.T) {
   125		repoDir := setupTestGitRepo(t)
   126	
   127		t.Run("returns repo root from root dir", func(t *testing.T) {
   128			root := FindProjectRoot(repoDir)
   129			assert.Equal(t, repoDir, root)
   130		})
   131	
   132		t.Run("returns repo root from subdirectory", func(t *testing.T) {
   133			subDir := filepath.Join(repoDir, "sub", "deep")
   134			require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(subDir, 0755))
   135			root := FindProjectRoot(subDir)
   136			assert.Equal(t, repoDir, root)
   137		})
   138	
   139		t.Run("returns input for non-git directory", func(t *testing.T) {
   140			nonGit := t.TempDir()
   141			root := FindProjectRoot(nonGit)
   142			assert.Equal(t, nonGit, root)
   143		})
   144	}

   146	// TestFindNearestDDxWorkspace_LinkedWorktreePrefersPrimary verifies that
   147	// FindNearestDDxWorkspace resolves to the PRIMARY worktree's .ddx/ when
   148	// called from inside a linked worktree, even if the linked worktree itself
   149	// has its own .ddx/ directory. This protects bead store mutations from
   150	// silently landing in an ephemeral execution worktree (ddx-381f4171).
   151	func TestFindNearestDDxWorkspace_LinkedWorktreePrefersPrimary(t *testing.T) {
   152		primary := setupTestGitRepo(t)
   153	
   154		// Create the primary's .ddx/ with a marker file so we can tell them apart.
   155		primaryDdx := filepath.Join(primary, ".ddx")
   156		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(primaryDdx, 0755))
   157		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(primaryDdx, "marker.txt"), []byte("primary"), 0644))
   158	
   159		// Add a linked worktree as a sibling of the primary.
   160		linked := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(primary), "linked-wt")
   161		runGitInDir(t, primary, "worktree", "add", "-b", "linked-branch", linked)
   162	
   163		// Create the linked worktree's own .ddx/ — this is the trap. Without the
   164		// fix, FindNearestDDxWorkspace would return the linked dir.
   165		linkedDdx := filepath.Join(linked, ".ddx")
   166		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(linkedDdx, 0755))
   167		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(linkedDdx, "marker.txt"), []byte("linked"), 0644))
   168	
   169		// From inside the linked worktree, we must resolve to the PRIMARY.
   170		got := FindNearestDDxWorkspace(linked)
   171		assert.Equal(t, primary, got, "expected primary worktree root, got linked worktree")
   172	
   173		// From a subdir of the linked worktree, same result.
   174		subdir := filepath.Join(linked, "some", "deep", "dir")
   175		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(subdir, 0755))
   176		got = FindNearestDDxWorkspace(subdir)
   177		assert.Equal(t, primary, got, "expected primary worktree root from subdir of linked worktree")
   178	
   179		// From inside the primary, resolve to the primary as before.
   180		got = FindNearestDDxWorkspace(primary)
   181		assert.Equal(t, primary, got)
   182	}

    29	// TestAcceptance_US008_CheckDDxVersion tests US-008: Check DDX Version
    30	func TestAcceptance_US008_CheckDDxVersion(t *testing.T) {
    31		t.Run("TestRootVersionFlag_PrintsVersionAndBuildInfo", func(t *testing.T) {
    32			rootCmd := getVersionTestRootCommand("")
    33			output, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "--version")

    36			assertVersionInfo(t, output)

    40			rootCmd := getVersionTestRootCommand("")
    41			output, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "version")
    42	
    43			require.NoError(t, err, "Version command should execute successfully")
    44			assertVersionInfo(t, output)
    45	
    46			// Should follow semantic versioning pattern (at least major.minor.patch)
    47			lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
    48			versionLine := ""
    49			for _, line := range lines {
    50				if strings.Contains(line, "DDx") && strings.Contains(line, "v") {
    51					versionLine = line
    52					break
    53				}
    54			}
    55			assert.NotEmpty(t, versionLine, "Should find version line")
    56			assert.Contains(t, versionLine, ".", "Version should contain dots (semantic versioning)")
    57		})
    58	
    59		t.Run("include_build_information", func(t *testing.T) {
    60			// AC: Given version is displayed, when I view the output, then build information (commit hash, build date) is included
    61			rootCmd := getVersionTestRootCommand("")

    63	
    64			require.NoError(t, err, "Version command should work")
    65

    68	
    69			// Should include build date
    70			assert.Contains(t, output, "Built:", "Should show build date")
    71	
    72			// Verify format of build information
    73			lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
    74			hasCommit := false
    75			hasBuilt := false
    76	
    77			for _, line := range lines {
    78				if strings.Contains(line, "Commit:") {
    79					hasCommit = true
    80					// Commit hash should be non-empty
    81					parts := strings.Split(line, ":")
    82					if len(parts) >= 2 {
    83						hash := strings.TrimSpace(parts[1])
    84						assert.NotEmpty(t, hash, "Commit hash should not be empty")
    85						assert.Greater(t, len(hash), 6, "Commit hash should be reasonably long")
    86					}
    87				}
    88				if strings.Contains(line, "Built:") {
    89					hasBuilt = true
    90					// Build date should be in reasonable format
    91					parts := strings.Split(line, ":")
    92					if len(parts) >= 2 {
    93						date := strings.TrimSpace(parts[1])

    96						if date != "unknown" {
    97							assert.Contains(t, date, "2025", "Build date should be current year")
    98						}
    99					}
   100				}
   101			}
   102	
   103			assert.True(t, hasCommit, "Should have commit information")
   104			assert.True(t, hasBuilt, "Should have build date information")
   105		})

ID:       ddx-633b16eb
Title:    cli: 'ddx --version' should warn when installed binary is behind source tree
Type:     bug
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, area:cli, kind:fix, observed-failure, reliability, bead-quality, triage:no-changes-unjustified
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-e34994e2
Desc:     PROBLEM
After rebuilding ddx (make install), the installed binary at ~/.local/bin/ddx is correct. But subsequent commits land in the source tree, and the operator forgets to rebuild. New flags / subcommands appear in source but silently disappear from the operator's CLI surface. Today's session bit this twice: 'ddx work plan --limit' worked, then later returned 'unknown flag: --limit' — not because the feature was broken, but because the binary was stale relative to source.

OBSERVED (2026-05-03 session)
- Built + installed at commit c0f95380; 'ddx work plan --limit' worked.
- ~10 commits later: 'ddx work plan --limit' returned 'unknown flag.'
- Source still had the flag definition; binary was stale.
- Operator wasted time investigating a non-existent regression before remembering to rebuild.

This compounds because:
1. Source can drift hours-to-days ahead of the installed binary during active development.
2. Cobra's 'unknown flag' / 'unknown command' errors give NO hint that the binary is stale.
3. ddx work auto-spawns workers — those workers also use the stale binary, propagating the gap.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/version.go implements 'ddx version' and 'ddx --version'. It prints the build-time-injected version string (e.g., 'v0.6.2-alpha12-12-g86848d21') from ldflags but does NOT compare against the source tree's current HEAD when invoked from inside a ddx project repo. No stale-binary detection exists in cli/cmd/doctor.go either. The build SHA is injected at link time via main.go -ldflags but never compared against runtime git rev-parse HEAD.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend 'ddx --version' (or add 'ddx doctor' check) to:
1. Detect when invoked from inside a git repo whose origin matches the ddx project (DocumentDrivenDX/ddx).
2. Compare the binary's build SHA (from -ldflags / main.go version injection) against the repo's HEAD.
3. If HEAD is ahead of the binary's SHA, print a warning to stderr: 'WARNING: installed ddx is built from <binary-sha>; source tree HEAD is <head-sha> (<N> commits ahead). Run "make install" to refresh.'
4. Exit 0 (informational, not fatal).

Optional: emit same warning on EVERY ddx command invocation when inside the ddx project (cheap: one git rev-parse).

NON-SCOPE
- Auto-rebuilding (operator should explicitly rebuild).
- Comparing against upstream remote (only local source tree).
- Detection from outside the ddx project repo.
Accept:   1. cli/cmd/version.go or cli/cmd/doctor.go detects when invoked from inside a git repo whose origin matches DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.
2. When stale (HEAD ahead of build SHA), prints to stderr: 'WARNING: installed ddx is built from <binary-sha>; source tree HEAD is <head-sha> (<N> commits ahead). Run "make install" to refresh.'
3. Exit code 0 (informational only).
4. TestVersion_StaleWarning_PrintsToStderr verifies warning appears when HEAD is ahead of injected SHA.
5. TestVersion_NoWarningWhenInSync verifies no warning when HEAD matches build SHA.
6. TestVersion_NoWarningOutsideDDxRepo verifies no warning when invoked from a non-ddx repo.
7. TestVersion_HandlesDetachedHead verifies graceful handling of detached HEAD (warning OK or skipped).
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

data.output:
ID:       ddx-633b16eb
Title:    cli: 'ddx --version' should warn when installed binary is behind source tree
Type:     bug
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, area:cli, kind:fix, observed-failure, reliability, bead-quality, triage:no-changes-unjustified
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-e34994e2
Desc:     PROBLEM
After rebuilding ddx (make install), the installed binary at ~/.local/bin/ddx is correct. But subsequent commits land in the source tree, and the operator forgets to rebuild. New flags / subcommands appear in source but silently disappear from the operator's CLI surface. Today's session bit this twice: 'ddx work plan --limit' worked, then later returned 'unknown flag: --limit' — not because the feature was broken, but because the binary was stale relative to source.

OBSERVED (2026-05-03 session)
- Built + installed at commit c0f95380; 'ddx work plan --limit' worked.
- ~10 commits later: 'ddx work plan --limit' returned 'unknown flag.'
- Source still had the flag definition; binary was stale.
- Operator wasted time investigating a non-existent regression before remembering to rebuild.

This compounds because:
1. Source can drift hours-to-days ahead of the installed binary during active development.
2. Cobra's 'unknown flag' / 'unknown command' errors give NO hint that the binary is stale.
3. ddx work auto-spawns workers — those workers also use the stale binary, propagating the gap.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/version.go implements 'ddx version' and 'ddx --version'. It prints the build-time-injected version string (e.g., 'v0.6.2-alpha12-12-g86848d21') from ldflags but does NOT compare against the source tree's current HEAD when invoked from inside a ddx project repo. No stale-binary detection exists in cli/cmd/doctor.go either. The build SHA is injected at link time via main.go -ldflags but never compared against runtime git rev-parse HEAD.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend 'ddx --version' (or add 'ddx doctor' check) to:
1. Detect when invoked from inside a git repo whose origin matches the ddx project (DocumentDrivenDX/ddx).
2. Compare the binary's build SHA (from -ldflags / main.go version injection) against the repo's HEAD.
3. If HEAD is ahead of the binary's SHA, print a warning to stderr: 'WARNING: installed ddx is built from <binary-sha>; source tree HEAD is <head-sha> (<N> commits ahead). Run "make install" to refresh.'
4. Exit 0 (informational, not fatal).

Optional: emit same warning on EVERY ddx command invocation when inside the ddx project (cheap: one git rev-parse).

NON-SCOPE
- Auto-rebuilding (operator should explicitly rebuild).
- Comparing against upstream remote (only local source tree).
- Detection from outside the ddx project repo.
Accept:   1. cli/cmd/version.go or cli/cmd/doctor.go detects when invoked from inside a git repo whose origin matches DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.
2. When stale (HEAD ahead of build SHA), prints to stderr: 'WARNING: installed ddx is built from <binary-sha>; source tree HEAD is <head-sha> (<N> commits ahead). Run "make install" to refresh.'
3. Exit code 0 (informational only).
4. TestVersion_StaleWarning_PrintsToStderr verifies warning appears when HEAD is ahead of injected SHA.
5. TestVersion_NoWarningWhenInSync verifies no warning when HEAD matches build SHA.
6. TestVersion_NoWarningOutsideDDxRepo verifies no warning when invoked from a non-ddx repo.
7. TestVersion_HandlesDetachedHead verifies graceful handling of detached HEAD (warning OK or skipped).
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
